records.specific_details: Planning and Development Bill 2025
records.intended_results: To highlight concerns  and the risks given the Government's approach to rushing Planning legislation through the Oireachtas seeking to fix errors made in the rushed Planning and Development Act 2024 and that is non-compliant with the gov amendments with EU Law and Aarhus Convention. Additionally highlight isses with delay in publishing key statuory instrument and delay in publishing on the eISB

records.specific_details: Input into Housing Committee Work Programme
records.intended_results: Sought for the Housing Committee to prioritise sessions on implementing minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement and tenant protections against renovictions, and on scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes with multi-annual funding and a focus on worst-performing stock, to ensure climate targets are met while protecting tenants and reducing energy poverty.
lobbying_activities: Called for the Housing Committee to hold sessions on:Implementation of minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement, and tenant protections against renovictions.Scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes, with a focus on prioritising the worst-performing stock and shifting to multi-annual funding.Provided supporting evidence from ESRI analysis, Department consultations, and NGO research. - Email
Met with Deputy Hearne outlining the same - Meeting

records.specific_details: Advocacy on Data Centre Moratorium Motion in South Dublin County Council
records.intended_results: The passing of a motion in South Dublin County Council that called on the Minister for Climate, Environment and Energy to introduce a national moratorium on data centres.
lobbying_activities: Email to Cllr Roisín Mannion inviting her to South Dublin County Partnership workshop on data centres. - Email

Emails to all councillors in South Dublin County Council encouraging them to support the motion tabled by Cllr Jess Spear: “That this council resolves to write to the Minister for Climate and the Environment requesting a national moratorium on further data centre expansion.” - Email
Phone Calls with Cllr Jess Spear on data centre moratorium motion. - Phone call
Phone call with Cllr Helen Farrell on 14/07 on amendment to data centre moratorium motion. - Phone call

Three meetings with Cllr Jess Spear between June 30th and September 8th regarding her motion to write to the Minister for Climate and Environment requesting a national moratorium on data centre development.  Two meetings were virtual and one was at Bawnogue Enterprise Centre. - Meeting
Whatsapp messages with Cllr Jess Spear on data centre moratorium motion. - Informal communication

lobbying_activities: Emailed Housing Minister James Browne's office on [6 May] to follow up on request sent (25 April) inviting him to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May at Buswells Hotel. Requested that the invite be forwarded on to another Junior Minister in DHLGH if Minister Browne was unable to attend. - Email

Emailed Minister of State Kieran O'Donnell [13 May,19 May, 23 May] to follow up on request for him to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May. - Email

Emailed several DPOs on [4 June] who had been invited to, but were unable to attend the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum breakfast event to provide them with a brief recap of the event and attach some photos. - Email

Emailed the office of Minister of State John Cummins on the [7 May] to thank them for considering our request for Minister Cummins to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May. Requested that the Minister of State for Older People and Housing Kieran O'Donnell. - Email

Held an event in Buswells Hotel on [May 27] with over 40 attendees. The Healthy Homes Ireland Forum breakfast event brought together key stakeholders to consider how occupants can be empowered to operate healthy and energy efficient homes. The Irish Green Building Council's Home User Guide was launched at the event and there followed a panel discussion with experts on the importance of a healthy indoor environment and how householders can be empowered to run a healthy home. - Event / Reception
On [10 June, 9 July] emailed Colm Burke TD to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [12 June] emailed Micheal Carrigy TD, Chair of the  Joint Oireachtas Committee on Housing, Local Government and Heritage to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [12 June] emailed Naoise O' Muirí TD, Chair of the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Climate, Environment and Energy to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Rory Hearne TD, to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June, 30 July, 4 August, 6 August] emailed Pa Daly TD to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [18 June] emailed Conor Sheehan TD to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [18 June] emailed Jen Cummins TD to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June] emailed Minister Jennifer Murnane O'Connor to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations in the Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June] emailed Minister of State Kieran O'Donnell's office to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Joint letter from the PSI and allied professional bodies to TDs outlining concerns re the HSE’s draft Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol, including Tier 1 assessments, alignment with best practice, and calling for urgent engagement with the HSE.
records.intended_results: PSI and other professional bodies circulated a joint letter originally sent to John Fitzmaurice of the HSE to Oireachtas members to raise concerns re the HSE’s draft Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol. The activity aimed to ensure elected representatives were informed of professional positions, highlight issues around Tier 1 assessments and adherence to guidelines, and encourage engagement with the HSE to support safe, evidence-based, and effective implementation in the public interest.
lobbying_activities: Circulation of the joint letter from Odhrán Allen (AOTI), signed by multiple professional bodies including PSI, to TDs Liam Quaide and Eoin O’Broin regarding the Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol and requesting engagement with the HSE. - Informal communication
